pimpQuestions: Q: What complication is more common utilizing a posterior THA approach? A: Dislocation

Q: What are the steps involved in the posterior (Southern-Moore) approach to the hip? A: 1) Split the gluteus maximus (inferior gluteal nerve) 2) Retract the gluteus medius anteriorly 3) Identify piriformis and short external rotators 4) Detach external rotators 5) Dislocate the hip
Q: What is the femoral component of a THA implant should be placed with how much anteversion? A: 15°
Q: What are the three most common bearing combinations for THA? A: Ceramic on polyethylene, Ceramic on ceramic, Metal (cobalt-chrome) on polyethylene
Q: What symptoms suggest a loose femoral component following THA? A: Thigh pain, Start-up pain with walking
otherUsefulFacts: Pseudotumors are a complication seen with metal on metal THA bearing surfaces.
Excessively increased femoral head offset can lead to trochanteric bursitis.
The hip should be fused in 25-30 degrees of flexion, 0 degrees of abduction and rotation.

selected.rationale: Posterior total hip arthroplasty is best performed using the posterior (Moore/Southern) approach, which provides direct access to the hip joint from the posterior side and is commonly used for this procedure.

approachSelection.notes: Only one approach from the allowed set fits the posterior total hip arthroplasty anatomy and surgical goals.

questions.q: In the posterior hip (Moore/Southern) approach, what two structures define the superficial interval?
questions.answer: The superficial interval is between the gluteus maximus and the iliotibial band (IT band).

questions.q: During the posterior hip approach, which muscles form the deep interval used for exposure?
questions.answer: The deep interval is between the piriformis and short external rotators and the femur.

questions.q: What is a major nerve at risk during the posterior hip (Moore/Southern) surgical approach?
questions.answer: The sciatic nerve is at risk during this approach.

questions.q: Which artery is considered at risk in the posterior hip approach described by Moore/Southern?
questions.answer: The inferior gluteal artery is at risk.

questions.q: In the posterior hip approach, what is the significance of the MFCA?
questions.answer: The medial femoral circumflex artery (MFCA) is at risk and important for femoral head blood supply.

questions.q: Which bones are primarily exposed using the posterior hip (Moore/Southern) approach?
questions.answer: The femur and the pelvis can be exposed through this approach.

questions.q: The posterior hip (Moore/Southern) approach is primarily used to access which joint?
questions.answer: It provides access to the hip joint.

questions.q: Name one common surgical alias for the posterior hip approach described.
questions.answer: Moore approach (also known as Southern approach or posterior hip approach).
